rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 Октоїха, 3 мч., Сл., І нині: хрестобогород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар мч. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мч. Катизми черг., сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон Октоїха і мч. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: світильний мч., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мч.
Часи: на 1-му – дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – мч.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – 1 Кор. 130 зач.; 4, 5-8. Єв. – Мт. 55 зач.; 13, 44-54.

troparion_en.text: Like a fragrant hyacinth of the Church of Christ, O all-blessed Hyacinth, / you radiate grace to the ends of the world. / By the brilliance of your confession of faith, / you were illustrious in contest in emulation of God the Word / and you ever illumine those who acclaim you.

kontakion_en.text: Come, you faithful, / plait a crown of unfading hyacinths today for the Martyr Hyacinth, / and let us cry to Him: / “Rejoice, glory of martyrs.”

matched_oca_saint: Martyr Hyacinth of Caesarea, in Cappadocia, and those with him

oca_saints.name: Martyr Hyacinth of Caesarea, in Cappadocia, and those with him

troparion.text: Like a fragrant hyacinth of the Church of Christ, O all-blessed Hyacinth, / you radiate grace to the ends of the world. / By the brilliance of your confession of faith, / you were illustrious in contest in emulation of God the Word / and you ever illumine those who acclaim you.

kontakion.text: Come, you faithful, / plait a crown of unfading hyacinths today for the Martyr Hyacinth, / and let us cry to Him: / “Rejoice, glory of martyrs.”

oca_saints.name: Translation of the relics of Hieromartyr Philip, Metropolitan of Moscow

troparion.text: O foremost pillar of Orthodoxy / and defender of the truth, / the new confessor Philip laid down his life for his flock / therefore having boldness before Christ our God / pray for this city and people who honor your holy memory.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Anatolius of the Kiev Near Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Anatolius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Anatolius the Recluse of the Kiev Far Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Anatolius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

At first you were honored with the episcopal dignity at Murom, but were unjustly banished.  / You climbed
onto your mantya as if it were a light ship; /  and as one without a body, you made your way supernaturally
over the water, /  guided by the Providence of the Most High, and by the intercession of the Mother of God,
you reached the God-saved city of Ryazan. /  There you accepted your hierarchal throne, and your God-given
flock of Ryazan.  / You have done well, O Hierarch and Father Basil.  / Entreat Christ God that our souls may
be saved.

kontakion.text: O Hierarch Basil, you shone in the city of Murom like a bright star,  /  and upon the water you reached the city of Ryazan; /   you helped the Church of God well by enlightening the people with your teaching and your life.  /  Even now, remember your children who pray to you, our good teacher, /   and entreat Christ God and His Most Pure Mother for us, /   that with grateful souls we may cry to you:  /  "Rejoice, O Christ-pleaser and fervent intercessor with God for us.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Longinus, Wonderworker of Yarenga, Solovki

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Longinus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Νikόdēmos, Abbot of Kozhe Lake

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Νikόdēmos, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

Your holy martyrs Mark and Mocius, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Saint Alexander, founder of the Monastery of the “Unsleeping Ones”

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Alexander, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Anatolius, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

oca_saints.name: Monastic Martyr Gerasimus the New of Carpenision

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “The entire world has found you to be a great champion in times of peril...”1)
O gift of honor to the Church,  / divinely bright flame of Evrytania, and boast of the New Martyrs, /  you longed for Christ through asceticism and confessed God by martrydom.  / O Monastic Martyr Gerasimos, /  through the intercessions of the Mother of God, entreat Him that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the things on high...”)
You were made manifest by your splendor as a bright star,  / proclaiming Christ to the temporal world, O Martyr Gerasimos,  / and by vanquishing the allurement of false gods,  / you granted true light to the faithful, /  always interceding on behalf of us all.

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God the “Milk-Giver” of the Hilandar Monastery on Mount Athos

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “O citizen of the desert...”)
We bow down and glorify your holy Icon, O Virgin Milk-Giver, as the tabernacle of your glory;   /  from it you mystically gush forth the milk of immaterial gifts,   /  and you nourish the hearts and souls of the faithful who cry out to you:  / “Glory to your majesty, O Pure One.  /  Glory to your miracles.  /  Glory to your inexpressible kindness toward us.”

kontakion.text: Having purified the perception of our souls,  /  we behold on the Icon a wondrous mystery:  /  the Creator of the Universe and the Lord of the Powers on High is held in your embrace,   / and as a Child He nurses at your breast;   /  with fear and joy we fall down before you   / and our Savior Who was born of you, and let us cry out,  /  “Rejoice, O Sovereign Lady, the nourisher of our life.”1

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father George, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!
